front: What is the main thread in a Java application?
back: It is the single thread that begins execution when a Java program starts. It is the thread from which all other 'child' threads are spawned.

front: How can you obtain a reference to the main thread object?
back: By calling `Thread.currentThread()` from within the main method. This method returns a reference to the currently executing thread object.

front: What is the default name and priority of the main thread?
back: The main thread's default name is 'main' and its default priority is `Thread.NORM_PRIORITY` (which is typically a value of 5).

front: Why is it important for the main thread to be the last to finish execution?
back: Because the program terminates when the main thread ends, even if other child threads are still running. These child threads will be terminated abruptly.

front: What is the role of the main thread in the context of a GUI application (like Swing or JavaFX)?
back: It is often known as the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) in Swing or the JavaFX Application Thread. It is responsible for handling user interface events and painting the UI components.
